title: Infinity Inner Products and Open Gromov-Witten Invariants
speaker: Sebastian Haney
abstract: The open Gromov-Witten (OGW) potential is a function defined  on the Maurer-Cartan space of a closed Lagrangian submanifold in a  symplectic manifold with values in the Novikov ring. From the values  of the OGW potential, one can extract so-called open Gromov-Witten invariants, which count pseudoholomorphic disks with boundary on the Lagrangian. Standard definitions of the OGW potential only allow for  the construction of OGW invariants with values in the real or complex numbers. In this talk, we will present a construction of the OGW potential which gives invariants valued in any field of characteristic zero. The main algebraic input for our construction is a homotopy cyclic inner product on the (curved) Fukaya A-infinity algebra, which generalizes the notion of a cyclically symmetric inner product and is determined by a proper Calabi-Yau structure on the Fukaya category.

proof_stages.label: Naive disk counts and boundary-bubbling obstruction
proof_stages.summary: The talk sets up open Gromov-Witten invariants as counts of J-holomorphic disks with boundary on a closed Lagrangian, then explains that the naive zero-dimensional count is not invariant under changing J because one-parameter moduli spaces acquire sphere-bubbling and disk-bubbling boundary strata.

proof_stages.label: Curved Fukaya algebra and bounding cochains
proof_stages.summary: To encode and cancel disk bubbling, the speaker introduces Novikov coefficients, a chain model for C*(L), the curved A-infinity Fukaya operations whose m0 records disk bubbles, and bounding cochains satisfying the Maurer-Cartan equation so that the deformed operations become uncurved.

proof_stages.label: Classical open potential and cyclicity requirement
proof_stages.summary: The Fukaya open potential is defined by adding higher disk counts with boundary insertions from a bounding cochain to the naive count, and its invariance along paths of bounding cochains is shown to require cyclic symmetry of the Poincare pairing with the A-infinity operations.

proof_stages.label: Failure of strict cyclicity in ordinary chain models
proof_stages.summary: Although cyclic symmetry looks geometrically like rotation of boundary marked points on a disk, transversality forces domain-dependent perturbations, and the speaker explains why strict cyclicity is only known in the de Rham model over real-containing fields and why singular-chain fiber-product definitions fail.

proof_stages.label: Homotopy-cyclic infinity inner product on Morse cochains
proof_stages.summary: The cyclic pairing is replaced by an A-infinity bimodule quasi-isomorphism from the diagonal bimodule to the dual, constructed on the Morse cochain model by counts of pearly trees and interpreted as a chain-level homotopy-cyclic or Calabi-Yau structure under low-dimensional regularity assumptions.

proof_stages.label: Homotopy-cyclic open Gromov-Witten potential over characteristic zero
proof_stages.summary: Using the higher components of the infinity inner product, the speaker defines a revised open Gromov-Witten potential on the Morse model, invokes the algebraic homotopy-cyclic formalism for gauge invariance, and draws consequences about the fields in which the resulting invariants live.

examples.name: Lagrangian rational homology sphere
examples.note: A rational homology sphere Lagrangian illustrates that the characteristic-zero construction can yield genuinely rational open Gromov-Witten numbers via a minimal model with zero bounding cochain.
examples.engagement_reason: the speaker applies the theory to rational homology spheres, using the minimal-model/zero-bounding-cochain argument to conclude the invariants are rational
